zh.lifeSummary: Alice Lake於1595年出生在英國。她於1609年7月10日與John Browne在英國英格蘭倫敦結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1617年在她的家鄉享年22歲。

nameMeanings.name: Lake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly West Country): topographic name usually for someone who lived by a streamlet (Middle English lak(e), Old English lacu) or who lived at or came from any of the places so named, such as Lack in Church Stoke (Shropshire) and Lake in Wilsford near Amesbury (Wiltshire). Lake is a common minor placename in Devon.

English: occasionally perhaps a topographic name for someone who lived by a lake or pool (Middle English, Old French lake), though it is uncertain that this word was current in ordinary vocabulary during the main period of surname formation (1250–1400).

North German and Dutch: habitational name from any of several places in Westphalia and Lower Saxony so named, or a topographic name from Middle Low German, Middle Dutch lake ‘swamp, swampy meadow’ (Middle Dutch also ‘border water’).

variants.name: Westlake
meanings: English (southwestern): habitational name from Westlake in Ermington (Devon), or a topographic name for someone who lived to the west of a stream (Middle English west ‘west’ + Middle English lak(e), from Old English lacu ‘stream, watercourse’). See Lake .

variants.name: Lakeman
meanings: English: from Middle English lak(e) ‘stream’ (Old English lacu) or lake ‘lake’ (Old French lake, Latin lacus) + man, denoting a dweller by such a feature. Compare Lake .
Dutch: variant of Lake with the addition of man ‘man’.
Dutch: occupational name for someone who deals with laken ‘cloth’.

variants.name: Lack
meanings: North German: nickname from Middle Low German lak ‘lack, ailment, infirmity, blemish, flaw’.

variants.name: Laker
meanings: English (Sussex and Kent): topographic name for someone who lived by a stream, from Old English lacu ‘stream’ (see Lake , Lakeman ) + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: Alice Lake was born in 1595, in England. She married John Browne on 10 July 1609, in London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1617, in her hometown, at the age of 22.
